changes_summary: Reordered steps to fix FLAG-001: the BrowserRouter hoist (formerly Step 3) is now Step 1, and the useJoinClips migration (formerly Step 1) is now Step 3. This ensures useNavigate() has a router provider before useJoinClips switches to it, since AgentChat renders MediaLightbox which calls useJoinClips at render time — and AgentChat sits outside the old RouterProvider's context. Added explicit explanation of the ordering constraint in the Overview and Execution Order sections.
questions: The errorElement on most routes renders NotFoundPage. With BrowserRouter, these become unhandled render errors. Should we wrap route groups in a React ErrorBoundary (e.g., ToolErrorBoundary already exists for tool routes), or is removing errorElement acceptable since render errors will bubble to the nearest error boundary?

NotFoundPage currently displays useRouteError() details for both 404s and render errors. After migration it will only serve as a catch-all 404 page. Should we add a separate generic React ErrorBoundary at the app level for render errors, or is the existing ToolErrorBoundary sufficient?

success_criteria.criterion: AgentChat component has access to router context (useNavigate, useLocation work inside it)
All existing routes render the same pages as before migration
Auth redirect on / still redirects logged-in users to /tools
Catch-all * route renders NotFoundPage for unknown URLs
TypeScript compilation passes with no errors
All existing tests pass (updated as needed)
router.ts singleton is fully removed — no getRouter/setRouter anywhere
No data-router-only APIs remain (createBrowserRouter, RouterProvider, loader, errorElement, useRouteError)
Layout route still uses Outlet for nested child routes
Route file stays under ~150 lines after migration
Manual smoke test: navigate all major routes, open AgentChat, verify auth redirect and 404 page

assumptions: BrowserRouter should be placed in App.tsx above AppProviders so all providers (including AgentChatProvider) and AgentChat itself have router context.
The authRedirectLoader's synchronous behavior can be preserved as a component-level check since probeStoredSessionToken() is already synchronous — no async loader needed.

errorElement on individual routes can be dropped since ToolErrorBoundary already wraps tool pages, and a catch-all * route handles unknown paths. If a top-level error boundary is needed, it can be added later.
The getRouter() singleton in router.ts has no other consumers besides useJoinClips.ts — grep confirmed only one call site.
No other code depends on the @remix-run/router Router type or the data router APIs (loaderData, useLoaderData, etc.) — grep showed only router.ts imports from @remix-run/router.
